entity.summary: Ukraine-focused centralized cryptocurrency exchange established in 2014 that announced a planned closure after repeated attacks in May 2015 but later resumed and continues operating its official trading platform.
entity.notes: Independent exchange profiles identify a 2014 establishment year. Contemporaneous May 2015 reporting documented the founder's plan to close after repeated attacks, but the current official site and API remain accessible, so HEI records the announcement as a historical event rather than a terminal death marker.

events.title: BTC Trade UA established
events.description: BTC Trade UA began operating as a Ukrainian cryptocurrency exchange in 2014.
events.notes: Reviewed sources establish the year but not the exact day, so January 1 is used as a year-level placeholder.

events.title: BTC Trade UA founder announced plans to close the exchange
events.description: The exchange's founder announced plans to close BTC Trade UA after repeated attacks and operational pressure; the service later returned and is currently active.
events.notes: This is a historical shutdown announcement, not a terminal death event, because the official platform later resumed operation.

evidence.title: BTC Trade UA official website
evidence.notes: Official domain verifies the current BTC Trade UA identity and active exchange service.

evidence.title: CoinPaprika exchange reference for BTC Trade UA
evidence.notes: Independent exchange profile identifies BTC Trade UA as a Ukrainian exchange founded in 2014.

evidence.title: CoinGecko exchange reference for BTC Trade UA
evidence.notes: Independent exchange profile identifies BTC Trade UA as established in 2014 and registered in Ukraine.

evidence.title: BTC Trade UA API documentation
evidence.notes: Official API documentation corroborates an operating exchange service and supported trading pairs.

evidence.title: Ukrainian bitcoin exchange btc-trade.com.ua is closing
evidence.notes: Contemporaneous report on the founder's announced closure plan.

evidence.title: Ukrainian programmer plans to close bitcoin exchange after hacker attacks
evidence.notes: Contemporaneous report identifying repeated attacks as the reason for the announced closure plan.
